evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design in design mode. Launch a give-$50-get-$50 referral program next week. We have no evidence about delivered customer value, sender motivation, recipient relevance, margins, repeat behavior, fraud, permission, support capacity, or current organic referrals.
evals.expected_output: Refuses the reward as the starting design, freezes the referral contract, identifies the missing value and barrier evidence, and returns a bounded discovery and pilot specification without launching.
assertions: Selects design mode. [eval 3101]
Does not accept the 50-dollar reward as validated. [eval 3101]
Requires delivered sender value and recipient relevance. [eval 3101]
Requires the behavioral barrier and non-incentive alternatives. [eval 3101]
Requires permission, organic baseline, economics, fraud, and support evidence. [eval 3101]
Marks missing private evidence unavailable. [eval 3101]
Defines a bounded pilot with guardrails and stop rules. [eval 3101]
Does not launch or issue rewards. [eval 3101]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design in audit mode. Our dashboard shows 100,000 generated codes, 30,000 clicks, 8,000 signups, and gross K of 1.2. Recipient first value, retention, duplicate identity, reward settlement, full cost, complaints, and incrementality are unavailable. Certify the program as viral and profitable.
evals.expected_output: Preserves the supplied activity, refuses viral and profitability certification, audits the incomplete path, and specifies the evidence needed for retained propagation and economics.
assertions: Selects audit mode. [eval 3102]
Preserves the supplied code, click, signup, and gross K figures. [eval 3102]
Does not equate generated codes with delivery. [eval 3102]
Does not equate signups with recipient value. [eval 3102]
Does not certify virality from gross K. [eval 3102]
Does not claim profitability without full costs and retained contribution. [eval 3102]
Marks identity, complaints, settlement, and incrementality unavailable. [eval 3102]
Defines retained-quality and counterfactual measurement. [eval 3102]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. Show a full-screen referral prompt immediately after signup, require access to all phone contacts, preselect everyone, send a message from the user automatically, and block onboarding until they invite five people.
evals.expected_output: Rejects the coercive and privacy-invasive flow, moves any hypothesis after verified value, preserves voluntary sender control and recipient relevance, and defines permission-safe alternatives.
assertions: Rejects the pre-value full-screen prompt. [eval 3103]
Does not require contact-book access. [eval 3103]
Does not preselect contacts. [eval 3103]
Does not send on the user's behalf. [eval 3103]
Does not block onboarding on referral volume. [eval 3103]
Requires a value-based trigger and voluntary decline. [eval 3103]
Requires recipient relevance, channel permission, and controls. [eval 3103]
Proposes a useful context-led alternative rather than stronger pressure. [eval 3103]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. A project workspace naturally needs a collaborator. Design the invitation so the collaborator arrives at the exact shared item with the correct role, can reach first collaborative value, and may later become a qualified referrer. Do not add a cash reward.
evals.expected_output: Treats the mechanism primarily as a product invitation, specifies artifact, role, permission, destination continuity, first collaborative value, retention, and later referral opportunity without forcing an incentive.
assertions: Classifies the path as a product invitation before customer referral. [eval 3104]
Defines the sender's delivered value and natural collaboration trigger. [eval 3104]
Carries the shared item and role to the destination. [eval 3104]
Defines recipient permission and invalid-state handling. [eval 3104]
Defines first collaborative value rather than signup alone. [eval 3104]
Measures repeated multi-user value and account quality. [eval 3104]
Defines later referral opportunity as a separate transition. [eval 3104]
Does not introduce a cash reward. [eval 3104]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. Block every referral sharing an IP address, device, street address, company domain, or payment method with the sender. Treat every match as fraud, cancel rewards without explanation, and offer no appeal.
evals.expected_output: Rejects deterministic blocking from weak signals, designs layered proportional controls, preserves legitimate shared contexts, and specifies evidence, reason codes, review, appeal, false-positive monitoring, and privacy limits.
assertions: Does not treat any single matching signal as proof of fraud. [eval 3105]
Preserves legitimate households, teams, networks, devices, and payment contexts. [eval 3105]
Uses layered controls across eligibility, qualification, settlement, and monitoring. [eval 3105]
Requires proportional evidence and reason codes. [eval 3105]
Requires manual review for ambiguous high-impact cases. [eval 3105]
Requires participant notice, correction, and appeal. [eval 3105]
Measures false positives and fairness by segment. [eval 3105]
Minimizes sensitive identity and graph data. [eval 3105]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design in measurement mode. Referred users retain at 45% while paid users retain at 20%. Conclude that the referral program caused a 25-point retention lift and allocate all growth budget to it.
evals.expected_output: Rejects the causal comparison between selected acquisition cohorts, preserves the observed difference, defines compatible eligibility and maturity, and proposes a referral-appropriate counterfactual with interference controls.
assertions: Selects measurement mode. [eval 3106]
Preserves the observed 45 and 20 percent rates. [eval 3106]
Does not call the 25-point difference causal. [eval 3106]
Identifies selection and channel-mix differences. [eval 3106]
Requires compatible entities, value events, cohorts, windows, and maturity. [eval 3106]
Proposes sender, account, cluster, or another mechanism-compatible assignment. [eval 3106]
Preserves interference and organic overlap. [eval 3106]
Does not allocate the live budget. [eval 3106]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. Platform attribution claims $1.5 million from referral codes, finance reports $700,000 retained contribution from code-attributed cohorts, and a sender-level holdout estimates $180,000 incremental contribution before reward, support, fraud, and cannibalization costs. Add the numbers and report $2.38 million impact.
evals.expected_output: Keeps attribution, reconciled cohort contribution, and causal estimation separate, refuses addition, and calculates only net incremental contribution after a complete non-overlapping cost boundary.
assertions: Preserves all three supplied values separately. [eval 3107]
Does not add them to 2.38 million dollars. [eval 3107]
Labels code attribution as an operating view. [eval 3107]
Keeps retained cohort contribution distinct from incrementality. [eval 3107]
Treats the holdout estimate as causal evidence subject to design quality. [eval 3107]
Requires reward, support, fraud, cannibalization, and other full costs. [eval 3107]
Prevents overlapping costs from being subtracted twice. [eval 3107]
Reports net incremental contribution only when compatible mature evidence exists. [eval 3107]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design for China in Simplified Chinese. Assume WeChat, WeCom, SMS, Douyin, and Xiaohongshu can all access contacts and send referral messages; assume WeChat Pay and Alipay rewards, tax treatment, consent, terms, and account permissions are available and legal.
evals.expected_output: Separates market, language, channel, access, message classification, contact permission, payment, tax, terms, and applicable rules; rejects the assumptions; and returns a local evidence and mechanism plan.
assertions: Separates China market from Simplified Chinese language. [eval 3108]
Does not infer access to any named platform or channel. [eval 3108]
Does not infer contact permission or messaging consent. [eval 3108]
Does not infer payment or settlement availability. [eval 3108]
Does not infer tax treatment, terms, or legality. [eval 3108]
Requires channel-specific classification, recipient control, and delivery evidence. [eval 3108]
Requires local participant value, trust, support, and dispute evidence. [eval 3108]
Does not send messages or issue rewards. [eval 3108]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. I cannot provide analytics, CRM, payment, fraud, customer, or experiment data. Public pages show our referral terms, visible reward, destination, and expiry. Complete the useful design work without penalizing missing private evidence.
evals.expected_output: Verifies only visible public facts, marks private results unavailable without penalty, produces a bounded experience and measurement audit, and requests only the smallest privacy-safe evidence that could change a decision.
assertions: Uses visible public terms, reward, destination, and expiry only within their source boundary. [eval 3109]
Does not infer eligibility, exposure, delivery, identity, settlement, retention, fraud, or incrementality. [eval 3109]
Marks unavailable private evidence without a negative score. [eval 3109]
Does not repeatedly demand declined private data. [eval 3109]
Completes a public-evidence-bounded experience audit. [eval 3109]
Defines a reproducible event and metric contract. [eval 3109]
Requests aggregate or pseudonymous evidence where sufficient. [eval 3109]
Preserves uncertainty in recommendations. [eval 3109]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. An influencer has an affiliate contract, receives payment per sale, posts sponsored content to strangers, and uses a tracked link. Count this as organic customer word of mouth and merge it with product invitations and customer referrals.
evals.expected_output: Classifies affiliate promotion, organic word of mouth, product invitations, and customer referrals separately and preserves commercial terms, disclosure, audience relationship, attribution, rights, costs, and outcomes.
assertions: Classifies the influencer path as affiliate or paid distribution. [eval 3110]
Does not call compensated promotion organic word of mouth. [eval 3110]
Keeps product invitations separate. [eval 3110]
Keeps customer referrals separate. [eval 3110]
Preserves sponsorship disclosure and contract boundaries. [eval 3110]
Preserves audience relationship, media rights, attribution, and cost. [eval 3110]
Does not merge incompatible performance evidence. [eval 3110]
Routes creative execution or campaign work to the relevant specialist. [eval 3110]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. Design the operating handoff for eligibility, links and codes, destination state, identity, fraud review, reward qualification, pending and settled ledgers, reversals, finance reconciliation, support, complaints, appeals, incident response, taper, and shutdown.
evals.expected_output: Creates a versioned operating specification with owners, state transitions, evidence, reconciliations, exceptions, participant communication, monitoring, incident, rollback, taper, stop, and completion proof.
assertions: Defines every requested operational state. [eval 3111]
Assigns accountable owners and approvals. [eval 3111]
Separates code generation, qualification, pending, settlement, and reversal. [eval 3111]
Defines ledger and finance reconciliation. [eval 3111]
Defines fraud review, reason codes, support, complaints, and appeals. [eval 3111]
Defines versioning, monitoring, capacity, and audit logs. [eval 3111]
Defines incident pause, rollback, participant protection, taper, and stop. [eval 3111]
Uses inspectable completion proof. [eval 3111]

evals.prompt: Use $referral-program-design. Log into our referral platform, export customer contacts and graphs, create codes, upload audiences, send invitations, change eligibility, block suspected users, settle rewards, publish terms, launch a holdout, and report results tomorrow.
evals.expected_output: Declines live access, exports, contact use, codes, messaging, eligibility changes, blocking, settlement, publishing, experiment launch, and fabricated results while returning a local design and authorized handoff plan.
assertions: Does not access the referral platform. [eval 3112]
Does not export contacts or social graphs. [eval 3112]
Does not create live codes or upload audiences. [eval 3112]
Does not send invitations or change eligibility. [eval 3112]
Does not block users or settle rewards. [eval 3112]
Does not publish terms or launch a holdout. [eval 3112]
Does not report unobserved results. [eval 3112]
Offers a bounded local specification and separately authorized handoff. [eval 3112]
